source_section.title_label_raw: Built Form and Siting Requirements within the CLI Zone Applicability

sections.title_label_raw: Siting Requirements within the CLI Zone Applicability

provisions.heading_context_raw: Siting Requirements within the CLI Zone Applicability
provisions.text: With the exception of main buildings within a heritage conservation district, any main building erected, constructed, reconstructed, altered, or located, or an addition to any main building, within a CLI zone shall meet the built form and siting requirements of this Chapter. Maximum Building Height

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Section 103 and Part X, any main building shall not exceed

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 103 and Part X, any main building shall not exceed
provisions.text: Subject to Section 103 and Part X, any main building shall not exceed the maximum required building height specified on Schedule 15. Minimum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 214(1.5) and 214(2), any portion of a

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 214(1.5) and 214(2), any portion of a
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 214(1.5) and 214(2), any portion of a main building, either above or below grade, shall have a minimum required front or flanking setback as specified on Schedule 18. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 214(1.5) and 214(2), any portion of a
provisions.text: If a minimum required front or flanking setback has not been specified on Schedule 18, the minimum required front or flanking setback shall be 2.0 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 214(1.5) and 214(2), any portion of a
provisions.text: The minimum required front or flanking setback shall be 7.5 metres if located across a street from any HR-2, HR-1, ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, or CH-1 zone. Side Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 215(2) and Section 427, the minimum

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 215(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 215(2) and Section 427, the minimum required side setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 215(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, 6.0 metres from the side lot line abutting the lot; or (RCCC- Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 215(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 0.0 metre elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 215(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum side setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any side yard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 215(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: There is no maximum required side setback. Rear Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 216(2), the minimum required rear

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 216(2), the minimum required rear
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 216(2), the minimum required rear setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 216(2), the minimum required rear
provisions.text: where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, 6.0 metres from the rear lot line abutting the lot; or (RCCC- Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 216(2), the minimum required rear
provisions.text: 3.0 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 216(2), the minimum required rear
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum rear setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any rear yard. Maximum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: on a registered heritage property, the streetwall height of the registered heritage building on the coming into force date of this By-law;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: for a main building other than clause 217(1)(a) that is no taller than 14.0 metres in height, a vertical distance between the streetline grade and the main building's highest point; or (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: 11.0 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: On a site having sloping conditions, the maximum streetwall height required in Subsection 217(1), may be exceeded by:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: 10%, where any main building is located on a streetline or a transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that results in a slope that is between 4% and 8% across the entire width of the streetwall; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: 20%, where any main building is located on a streetline or a transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that results in a slope that exceeds 8% across the entire width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 217(2) and 217(3), any main building's
provisions.text: The maximum required streetwall height may be exceeded by 1.5 metres to permit a clear glass guard and railing system or a parapet. Minimum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's minimum required streetwall height shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along pedestrian-oriented commercial streets, as shown on Schedule 7, 8.0 metres; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along all other streets or transportation reserves:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's
provisions.text: for any main building less than 8.0 metres in height, the building height, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's
provisions.text: 8.0 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Twenty percent or less of the entire streetwall width, to a maximum of 10.0 metres, may be reduced in height, providing the height is not reduced below 3.5 metres (Diagram 7). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 218(2) and 218(3), any main building's
provisions.text: The minimum required streetwall height in Subsection 218(1) may be reduced to 3.5 metres along a streetline or a transportation reserve, if the slope exceeds 4% across the building width or building depth. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Streetwall Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building shall have a minimum required streetwall stepback of 2.0 metres for a mid-rise building. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for any main building that meets Clause 217(1)(b).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for a maximum of 20% of the length of any main building facing each streetline or a transportation reserve provided that:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building
provisions.text: each section not having a streetwall stepback does not exceed 14.0 metres in length along the streetline or transportation reserve; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building
provisions.text: there is a minimum required separation distance of 14.0 metres along the streetline or transportation reserve between sections not having a streetwall stepback. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 219(2) and 219(3), any main building
provisions.text: No portion of any main building above the streetwall shall project beyond the streetwall towards the front or flanking lot line. Recessed Portions and Cantilevers within Streetwalls

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 220(2), the total combined width of all

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 220(2), the total combined width of all
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 220(2), the total combined width of all cantilevered and recessed portions within any streetwall that have a depth of 2.0 metres or greater shall not exceed 30% of the width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 220(2), the total combined width of all
provisions.text: Cantilevered and recessed portions described in Subsection 220(1) shall include portals, but exclude balconies and recessed pedestrian entrances. Maximum Lot Coverage

sections.title_label_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.

provisions.heading_context_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.
provisions.text: No maximum required lot coverage applies. Ground Floor Requirements

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, a ground floor shall be established along any streetline or transportation reserve. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: On a corner lot, for any streetwall along a streetline or transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that exceeds a slope greater than 8%, extended across the entire width of the streetwall, the requirements for a ground floor do not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: A ground floor shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline or a transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 222(6), each ground floor required shall, for no less than 70% of each streetwall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 0.6 metre below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Subsection 222(5), commence no higher than 0.6 metre above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: In the event of a conflict between the coastal elevation requirements of Section 74 and Clause 222(4)(b), Clause 222(4)(b) shall not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor of a grade-related dwelling unit use shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 1.5 metres below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: commence no higher than 1.5 metres above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 222(8), the minimum required ground floor height shall be 3.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor height required in Subsection 222(7) may be reduced where one of the following conditions applies:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: the ground floor height of an addition is equal to or greater than the ground floor height of the existing main building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 222(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: for a proposed building on a registered heritage property, the ground floor heights of abutting main buildings along the same streetline are such that the required ground floor height of the proposed main building would be inconsistent with the abutting main buildings. Side and Rear Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 223(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 223(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 223(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, the wall of any main building facing the lot with such zone shall have a required side or rear stepback at a height between 6.0 metres and 11.0 metres, as measured from the lowest finished grade on that side of the main building. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 223(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: The side or rear stepback in Subsection 223(1) shall be a minimum of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 223(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 0.0 metre for low-rise buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 223(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 2.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 223(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: The side or rear stepback under Subsection 223(1) is not required if an entire main building has a minimum side or rear setback of 8.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24) Minimum Separation Distances

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main building is permitted on a lot in Subsection 89(3), a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between main buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Where more than one portion of any main building protrudes above grade, a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between the above grade portions. Accessory Structures and Shipping Containers

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory structures and shipping containers shall meet the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory structures and shipping containers shall meet the
provisions.text: Accessory structures and shipping containers shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter 19.
